title: Discussing movie without the complete picture
display_title: Discussing movie without the complete picture
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Discussing movie without the complete picture             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Nov 29, 2012 10:43 pm Split from the Misato Shinji thread And here we run into the other problem plaguing this whole forum....about how many of us have actually seen the movie? We're running on a few tidbits of information with no details. If I had sense I'd have stayed out of the conversations entirely but I can't help myself when these topics start eating at me. That anti-Christ remark was an exaggeration (I do that) but what I'm getting at is simple. How many people died in Third Impact? How many members of WILLE lost sons and daughters and fathers and mothers and so on from that catastrophe? Too many to count. Then they find out that the guy who (as Bagheera put it) "pulled the trigger" is right there in their midst. Shinji's lucky they didn't form a lynch mob after they extracted him from EVA-01. It's that time Touji punched him out all over again, on a larger

[]:             Quote Post by IDontWantNoScrub » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:19 am CyberXIII wrote: And here we run into the other problem plaguing this whole forum....about how many of us have actually seen the movie? We're running on a few tidbits of information with no details. If I had sense I'd have stayed out of the conversations entirely but I can't help myself when these topics start eating at me.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:26 am That's still only a quarter of the movie. We won't see any visual details until a camrip or something his the internet. With a series like this we need everything we can before we start making judgement calls. I realize the hypocrisy of that statement but I really can't bring myself to care at this time of the night.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 30, 2012 1:32 am If you guys want to start a thread about how we can talk about the movie without actually having seen it, go ahead, I'll split the relevant posts to that thread (seeing as how the forum has been stable enough recently to even be able to split threads). But I'll add that, having seen the movie, I don't feel that confident in any sort of detailed interpretation of any kind, since I'm only going off of a fuzzy memory where details aren't accurate. A general feeling of things is fine, discussion of details I won't do until I've got a copy with English subtitles that I can pause and rewind. That being said, it's not like there shouldn't be discussions about the more general aspects, but I don't think people can help forming a picture from the information that they've read, and from that (personal) picture, create interpretations and assign meaning to things. EDIT: Ok split it anyways

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Fri Nov 30, 2012 8:30 am Well when we actually see the movie, that might change some opinions or not. But for me that won't be before the actual English DVD release, and that won't be for a while. Till then this keeps us fans occupied.

[]:             Quote Post by vinicius0712 » Fri Nov 30, 2012 9:26 am Yeah... if only people that watched the movie could express their opinions, the forum would have much less activity. Thank God I know english well enough to understand it, if I had to wait for portuguese subs and dubs in Brazil, I'd have to wait like 3 years to have any chance to see the new movie and them talk about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 30, 2012 11:51 am Personally, I haven't been discussing Q too much since I can't make any real judgements or process my thoughts until I actually see it (camrip then BD is fine by me). I do like how things sound despite some minor nitpicks.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Nov 30, 2012 12:39 pm Having the translated dialogue and the audio track to go with has been an immense help, but, yeah... This being Eva and all, there's only so far conversation can reasonably go prior to everybody having access to the visual component. (Or even an ultra-crappy counterfeit version thereof.)

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Fri Nov 30, 2012 2:59 pm ...
